title: Replace hardcoded requeue intervals with constants
description:
# GitHub Issue #131: Replace hardcoded requeue intervals with named constants or CR configuration

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: Requeue durations are scattered as magic numbers: 15s in reconcile_secrets.go, 30s in reconcile_database.go, 60s in reconcile_bootstrap.go. Extract these into named constants in a shared location, and optionally allow override via the CR spec for environments where different intervals are appropriate (e.g., faster in CI, slower in production).

**Motivation**: Magic numbers make behavior opaque and tuning difficult. Different environments benefit from different requeue intervals — CI needs fast convergence while production benefits from gentler polling.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All requeue intervals are defined as named constants in a single location
- [ ] Constants have descriptive names explaining the rationale for each interval
- [ ] Optionally: intervals are overridable via CR spec
- [ ] No behavioral change with default values

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/131

stories.title: Operator developer can identify requeue intervals by name
stories.want: all requeue intervals to be defined as named constants with descriptive names
stories.so_that: I can understand the rationale for each interval without reading scattered code across multiple files
criteria: All requeue intervals (10s, 15s, 30s, 60s) are defined as named constants in a single file within the controller package
Each constant has a descriptive name that conveys why that specific duration was chosen (e.g., RequeueSecretsPolling, RequeueDatabaseWait)
No hardcoded time.Second multiplications remain in reconcile_*.go production code
Constants are package-level (unexported is acceptable since they are internal)

stories.title: Operator developer can tune requeue intervals in one place
stories.want: to change a requeue interval by editing a single constant definition
stories.so_that: tuning does not require hunting through multiple files and risking inconsistent changes
criteria: Changing a constant value in the constants file is the only edit needed to change all usages of that interval
Test assertions reference the same constants so they remain correct after tuning
The integration_test.go eventuallyLongTimeout constant references the database requeue constant rather than a hardcoded value

stories.title: No behavioral change with default values
stories.want: the operator to behave identically after the refactoring
stories.so_that: this change is a safe, non-breaking improvement
criteria: All existing unit tests pass without modification to their assertions (only the interval references change)
All existing integration tests pass without behavioral changes
The reconcile loop requeues at exactly the same intervals as before: 15s for secrets, 30s for database, 60s for bootstrap, 10s for deployment

requirements.description: All requeue intervals in reconcile_secrets.go, reconcile_database.go, reconcile_bootstrap.go, and reconcile_deployment.go SHALL be replaced with named constants defined in a single file
requirements.rationale: Magic numbers scattered across 4 files make behavior opaque. Centralizing them in one file makes tuning and auditing straightforward.

scenarios.name: Secrets requeue uses named constant
scenarios.when: reconcileSecrets returns a requeue result because a secret is not ready
scenarios.then: the RequeueAfter value equals the RequeueSecretPolling constant (15s)
and_then: the literal '15 * time.Second' does not appear in reconcile_secrets.go

scenarios.name: Database requeue uses named constant
scenarios.when: reconcileDatabase returns a requeue result because a MariaDB CR or db_sync job is not ready
scenarios.then: the RequeueAfter value equals the RequeueDatabaseWait constant (30s)
and_then: the literal '30 * time.Second' does not appear in reconcile_database.go

scenarios.name: Bootstrap requeue uses named constant
scenarios.when: reconcileBootstrap returns a requeue result because the bootstrap job is in progress
scenarios.then: the RequeueAfter value equals the RequeueBootstrapWait constant (60s)
and_then: the literal '60 * time.Second' does not appear in reconcile_bootstrap.go

scenarios.name: Deployment requeue uses named constant
scenarios.when: reconcileDeployment returns a requeue result because the deployment is not ready
scenarios.then: the RequeueAfter value equals the RequeueDeploymentPolling constant (10s)
and_then: the literal '10 * time.Second' does not appear in reconcile_deployment.go

requirements.description: Named constants SHALL have descriptive names that explain the semantic purpose of each interval, and SHALL preserve the existing default values exactly
requirements.rationale: Descriptive names make the code self-documenting. Preserving exact values ensures zero behavioral change.

scenarios.name: Constants convey semantic meaning
scenarios.when: a developer reads the constants file
scenarios.then: each constant name indicates which sub-reconciler uses it and the nature of the wait (e.g., polling for external resource readiness vs waiting for job completion)

scenarios.name: Default values match original hardcoded values
and_then: all existing tests pass without changing expected values

requirements.description: Test assertions SHALL reference the named constants instead of hardcoded duration values
requirements.rationale: If tests use hardcoded values, changing a constant would cause test failures rather than automatically adapting. Tests must reference the same constants so they remain correct after tuning.

scenarios.name: Unit test assertions use constants
scenarios.when: a unit test asserts on a RequeueAfter value
scenarios.then: the expected value is the named constant, not a hardcoded duration literal
and_then: no hardcoded '15 * time.Second', '30 * time.Second', '60 * time.Second', or '10 * time.Second' assertions remain in reconcile_*_test.go files

scenarios.name: Integration test timeouts reference constants
scenarios.then: it is derived from RequeueDatabaseWait (e.g., 2*RequeueDatabaseWait) rather than a standalone hardcoded 60s
and_then: if RequeueDatabaseWait is tuned, the integration test timeout adjusts accordingly

requirements.description: The constants SHALL be defined in a dedicated file within the controller package and SHALL pass golangci-lint
requirements.rationale: A dedicated file keeps the constants discoverable and grouped. The code must pass the project linting configuration.

scenarios.name: Constants in dedicated file
scenarios.when: a developer looks for requeue interval definitions
scenarios.then: all requeue duration constants are defined in a single file (e.g., requeue_intervals.go) within the controller package
and_then: the file contains only the constants and their documentation, not reconciliation logic

scenarios.name: Lint passes
scenarios.when: golangci-lint is run on the controller package
scenarios.then: no new lint errors or warnings are introduced

scenarios.name: go vet passes
scenarios.when: go vet is run on the controller package
scenarios.then: no new vet issues are introduced

tasks.title: Define named requeue interval constants in a dedicated file (CC-0044, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/requeue_intervals.go with package-level constants: RequeueSecretPolling (15s — short interval for polling ESO-managed secret readiness), RequeueDatabaseWait (30s — moderate interval for MariaDB CR readiness and db_sync job completion), RequeueBootstrapWait (60s — longer interval for bootstrap job completion, which is a one-time heavyweight operation), RequeueDeploymentPolling (10s — shortest interval for deployment readiness, which should converge quickly). Include SPDX license header matching project convention. Each constant gets a doc comment explaining its purpose and rationale.

tasks.title: Replace hardcoded intervals in reconcile_secrets.go and update test assertions (CC-0044, REQ-001, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go: Replace all 4 instances of '15 * time.Second' (lines 42, 60, 76, 92) with RequeueSecretPolling. Remove 'time' from the import list since it will no longer be used. In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets_test.go: Replace all 5 instances of '15 * time.Second' in test assertions (lines 160, 197, 327, 368, 401) with RequeueSecretPolling. Verify the 'time' import can be removed from the test file if no other time references remain.

In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go: Replace all 5 instances of '30 * time.Second' in test assertions (lines 208, 229, 277, 312, 368) with RequeueDatabaseWait. tasks.title: Replace hardcoded intervals in reconcile_bootstrap.go and update test assertions (CC-0044, REQ-001, REQ-003)

Remove 'time' from the import list since it will no longer be used. In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go: Replace all 3 instances of '60 * time.Second' in test assertions (lines 133, 216, 255) with RequeueBootstrapWait. Verify the 'time' import can be removed from the test file if no other time references remain.

tasks.title: Replace hardcoded intervals in reconcile_deployment.go and update test assertions (CC-0044, REQ-001, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go: Replace the 1 instance of '10 * time.Second' (line 99) with RequeueDeploymentPolling. Check if 'time' can be removed from the import list (it may still be used elsewhere in this file). In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go: Replace all 5 instances of '10 * time.Second' in test assertions (lines 131, 155, 328, 520, 593) with RequeueDeploymentPolling. Verify the 'time' import can be removed from the test file if no other time references remain.

tasks.title: Update integration test timeout to derive from named constant (CC-0044, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go: Update the eventuallyLongTimeout constant (currently 60 * time.Second) to be derived from RequeueDatabaseWait (e.g., 2 * RequeueDatabaseWait) so that if the database requeue interval is tuned, the integration test timeout adjusts accordingly. The comment on eventuallyLongTimeout already explains it depends on the controller's 30s RequeueAfter delay — update the comment to reference the constant name instead. Leave eventuallyTimeout and pollInterval unchanged as they are independent of requeue intervals.

review_criteria: All 9 hardcoded requeue intervals in production code (4 in secrets, 3 in database, 1 in bootstrap, 1 in deployment) are replaced with named constants
All 18 hardcoded requeue intervals in test assertions (5 in secrets_test, 5 in database_test, 3 in bootstrap_test, 5 in deployment_test) are replaced with the same named constants
Constants file has SPDX license header matching project convention and descriptive doc comments for each constant
All existing unit tests pass with zero assertion changes (only reference changes from literals to constants)
golangci-lint and go vet pass without new issues on the controller package
No unused imports remain (time package removed from files where it is no longer needed)
integration_test.go eventuallyLongTimeout derives from RequeueDatabaseWait rather than a standalone 60s literal
implementation_notes:
This is a pure refactoring task — no behavioral changes, no API changes, no CRD changes.

**Architecture:** All constants are defined in a single new file `requeue_intervals.go` within the `controller` package (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/`). Since this is an internal package, the constants can be unexported (package-scoped). However, using exported names (e.g., `RequeueSecretPolling`) is fine and follows the pattern used for `subConditionTypes` in `keystone_controller.go`.

**Constant naming convention:** Use the pattern `Requeue{SubReconciler}{Semantic}` where:
- SubReconciler = Secret, Database, Bootstrap, Deployment
- Semantic = Polling (for readiness checks on external resources) or Wait (for completion of long-running operations)

**Interval values and rationale:**
- 10s (Deployment) — Deployment readiness converges quickly; frequent polling appropriate
- 15s (Secrets) — ESO secret sync is fast but depends on external vault; moderate polling
- 30s (Database) — MariaDB CR readiness and db_sync jobs are moderately slow; balance between responsiveness and API load
- 60s (Bootstrap) — Bootstrap is a one-time heavyweight operation; gentle polling sufficient

**Import cleanup:** After replacing `time.Second` literals, check each modified file to see if the `"time"` import can be removed. Files that still reference `time` for other purposes (e.g., `time.Now()`) should keep the import.

**Integration test coupling:** The `eventuallyLongTimeout` in `integration_test.go` is explicitly documented as depending on the 30s RequeueAfter delay. Changing it to `2 * RequeueDatabaseWait` makes this coupling explicit in code rather than just in comments.

**Files affected:**
- NEW: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/requeue_intervals.go` (constants definition)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go` (4 replacements)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go` (3 replacements)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go` (1 replacement)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` (1 replacement)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets_test.go` (5 replacements)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database_test.go` (5 replacements)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap_test.go` (3 replacements)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go` (5 replacements)
- MODIFY: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go` (1 timeout derivation)
